So end of day process is basically like your button down for the day, okay? You're going to batch all your admin activities so you're not doing it during your sales day, right? We talked about how you don't want to do admin during the day in the last video. You want to do it where when your willpower is low, you want to do sales when your willpower is high. So you have your binders or follow-ups, hot list, general list to make sure nothing's slipping through the cracks. And this is really important guys. The other part of that is just actually tracking it all correctly so you don't lose people because it's so easy when you got five calls a day every single day and you're contacting all these people, it's so easy for people to fall through the cracks. So why is this important to do daily? Well, again, perfect admin in the short term is really not that important for you as a sales rep, but in the long term it can be plus. ## [03:00 - 06:01]

So every single day I would look at like the five consults I had that were on my calendar and see is any of those people need to be on the hot list or general list. Then I would update my personal projection sheet, which was included in the Qs to high performance. I'd update the team tracking sheet. So I'd have my own personal tracking sheet where I'd track my numbers and then I'd have my team tracking sheet. Now if your team is using my tracking sheet, you'll kind of be able to have both of these in one. But still, even if you use my team tracking sheet, you can't track your projections. So I would still use both actually now that I think about it. I would use both the personal projection sheet that's included in Qs to high performance and you have to update your team's tracking sheet because otherwise your business owner is not going to know what's going on. You want to update your CRM. That means updating the deal notes from today's deals, noting each of the enrollments that you made, each of the consults that you had, all of that stuff. You want to create follow-up tasks from today's deals. So as you're going through and you're updating the deal stages, you're moving it, you're noting each of the consults you had, if there's a follow-up, usually there's a task function in the CRM. You can assign a task and a due date for whenever you want to follow up that person. You want to make sure that you update the enrollments that you made. So detailing the enrollment, the payment plan, what the person needs, just so that the client's access department or whoever that is is not in the dark. We want to make sure that you store all of today's call recordings if it's not automatic. So let's say you're using Dialpad and Drive. You have to store all of those manually.
